calendar.matters:
ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-21-2020 Off Calendar

Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Precision Parts And Machine
Off Calendar Oct-22-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Precision Parts and Machine.
Dept. 514. Judge: Gail Dekreon. Clerk: Kevin Dougherty, Reporter: Giselle Casey, CSR #8098. Telephone: 415-717-9768. Email: casey.giselle@gmail.com.

Geoff T. Sloniker, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Louis Dupont, who was not present at this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defendant Precision Parts and Machine.
Plaintiff's complaint for personal injury was filed on April 15, 2015.
Amendment to Complaint substituting Precision Parts and Machine for DOE Number 1 was filed May 18, 2015.
Plaintiff's Summons, Complaint and Amendment to Complaint were personally served on defendant Precision Parts and Machine's Authorized Agent for Service of Process on May 30, 2015.
Default as to defendant Precision Parts and Machine was entered on September 16, 2015.
Statment of Damages was filed on September 16, 2015.
Second Amended Proof of Service evidencing that plaintiff's Statement of Damages was personally served on defendant's Agent of Process on May 30, 2015, along with the Summons, Complaint and Amended to Complaint, was filed with the Clerk's Office on August 23, 2019.
Order waiving witness appearance at default prove-up hearing was filed on October 21, 2019.

After hearing the evidence presented, and good cause appearing, the Court rules in favor of Plaintiff Louis Dupont, and against the Defaulted Defendant Precision Parts and Machine, as follows:

Economic Damages - $929,691.14.
Non-Economic Damages - $10,000,000.00.
Total - $10,929,691.14.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Application For Entry Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Precision Parts And Machine
Off Calendar Aug-20-2019 Continued To Oct-22-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 514

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Maintaining The Current Trial Date With Preference
Off Calendar Mar-12-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
James P. Nevin of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and moving party Louis Dupont, Sr.
Nicole E. Gage of of TUCKER ELLIS LLP (415-617-2015), appearing for defendant Perfection Hy-Test Company.

RULING: PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER MAINTAINING THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE
No opposition and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff's motion to maintain the trial date with preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). No opposition filed. The February 27, 2017 trial date is maintained with preference status. The June 24, 2019 return holding date is vacated and the new return holding date is July 1, 2019 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is July 10, 2019. All pre-trial dates and deadlines remain dictated by the February 27, 2017 trial date. Plaintiff to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than May 17, 2019. Responses to the demand are due no later than May 31, 2019. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by June 21, 2019 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference with only the attorneys present and final decision makers available by phone is set for Thursday, June 27, 2019 at 9:30 a.m. in 603. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar Aug-19-2019 Set For Order To Show Cause On Feb-06-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503

DEPT 206 ASBESTOS TRIAL SETTING CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-21-2016 Set For Trial On Feb-27-17 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 503
